title: navarretia divaricata
intro: Navarretia divaricata, also known as spreading pincushion or mountain pincushion, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the western United States and parts of Canada. It is often used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Navarretia divaricata have been found to provide health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Navarretia divaricata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
